notes: DOCUMENTED TARGET (not yet recordable — see assessment.json: driver_capability gap:mid-turn-injection). Spec asserts: state stays working through the queued-message arrival, no →ready flicker when the queued input lands, no →waiting flicker, and the cycle still ends at ready once everything is consumed. opencode is the lone ProcessOwnedStore adapter — the daemon reads session state from the SQLite store keyed on ses_…, so a contiguous working span + clean working→ready close are the same store-read signals basic-turn proves. session_birth is anchored to recording start and left UNPINNED so a transient proc-<PID> presession cannot steal identity and cascade same_session_as failures; turn_start (matching working) establishes the session identity the later phases chain off. The queued_window phase pins the no-flicker invariant with duration_at_least_ms 3000. Whether the queued message folds into the current turn or is consumed as a follow-up turn is NOT asserted — both shapes finish in ready. NOT recordable today: drive-opencode-interactive.sh drives BLOCKING headless `opencode run` sequentially, so there is no in-flight turn to inject a second message into (gap:mid-turn-injection); this spec is the target for a future live-REPL opencode driver.

text: Session appears in ready within 2s of agent launch
PID bound to the ses_… session
First (slow) user prompt drives the ses_… session ready → working
A second user message is submitted mid-turn and queued. State must stay working on the same ses_… — no flicker to ready when the queued input lands, no flicker to waiting (spec bullets 1 and 2)
The cycle eventually ends at ready (spec bullet 3) — either the queued message folds into the current turn or is consumed as a follow-up turn; both shapes finish in ready on the same ses_…
